gandhi_quotes: Seek not greater wealth, but simpler pleasure; not higher fortune, but deeper felicity.
Your beliefs become your thoughts, Your thoughts become your words, Your words become your actions, Your actions become your habits, Your habits become your values, Your values become your destiny.
I do not want to foresee the future. I am concerned with taking care of the present. God has given me no control over the moment following.
Anger is the enemy of non-violence and pride is a monster that swallows it up.
Whenever you are confronted with an opponent. Conquer him with love.
It is easy enough to be friendly to one's friends. But to befriend the one who regards himself as your enemy is the quintessence of true religion. The other is mere business.
There's no God higher than truth.
It has always been a mystery to me how men can feel themselves honoured by the humiliation of their fellow beings.
My religion teaches me that whenever there is distress which one cannot remove, one must fast and pray.
There is no principle worth the name if it is not wholly good.
Even a single lamp dispels the deepest darkness.
There is no such thing as ‘too insane’ unless others turn up dead due to your actions.
Victory attained by violence is tantamount to a defeat for it is momen
What do I think of Western civilization? I think it would be a very good idea.
Morality is the basis of things and truth is the substance of all morality.
I know, to banish anger altogether from one's breast is a difficult task. It cannot be achieved through pure personal effort. It can be done only by God's grace.
In a gentle way, you can shake the world.

You don't know who is important to you until you actually lose them.

God has no religion.

On the contrary, if I have the belief that I can do it, I shall surely acquire the capacity to do it even if I may not have it at the beginning.

mandela_quotes: The education I received was a British education, in which British ideas, British culture, British institutions, were automatically assumed to be superior. There was no such thing as African culture.

I have retired, but if there's anything that would kill me it is to wake up in the morning not knowing what to do.
If you want to make peace with your enemy, you have to work with your enemy. Then he becomes your partner.

In the 1940s, traveling for an African was a complicated process. All Africans over the age of sixteen were compelled to carry 'Native passes' issued by the Native Affairs Department and were required to show that pass to any white policeman, civil servant, or employer. Failure to do so could mean arrest, trial, a jail sentence or fine.

I should tie myself to no particular system of society other than of socialism.

I have walked that long road to freedom. I have tried not to falter; I have made missteps along the way. But I have discovered the secret that after climbing a great hill, one only finds that there are many more hills to climb. I have taken a moment here to rest, to steal a view of the glorious vista that surrounds me, to look back on the distance I have come.
